text: What are the core physical/smothering characteristics of Carbon Dioxide (CO2) when used for firefighting?

options.text: It is in itself not dangerous (not toxic/poisonous in low levels)

explanation: Carbon Dioxide is an effective fire extinguishing agent because it is heavier than air, displaces oxygen, and is non-conductive, making it suitable for electrical fires; however, it is an asphyxiant requiring caution.
